codeReview.summary: Clean, correct implementation. A 256-entry compile-time LUT with boost=70 applies a non-linear coverage boost after rasterization, subpixel downsampling, and FIR filtering. The LUT preserves zero (special-cased) and 255, is monotonically non-decreasing, and strictly boosts mid-range values 1–251. Applied equally to all 3 subpixel channels via a simple loop over the output buffer. 7 well-structured tests cover boundary values, mid-range boost, monotonicity, tunability, integration with a rasterized glyph, and full formula verification. TDD workflow followed correctly (initial LUT[0] bug caught by failing test, then fixed). No bugs or missed edge cases. Two minor documentation inaccuracies noted as non-blocking.

issues.description: Doc comment claims 'LUT[c] > c for all c in 1..254 (strict boost)' but integer division truncation means LUT[252]=252, LUT[253]=253, LUT[254]=254 — these are NOT strictly boosted. The correct claim is 'LUT[c] > c for c in 1..251' and 'LUT[c] >= c for c in 252..254'. The test only checks 30–200 so this doesn't cause test failures, but the doc comment is misleading.

The LUT has a steep discontinuity: LUT[0]=0 but LUT[1]=70. Any pixel with raw coverage=1 jumps to 70 after darkening. This is inherent to the formula combined with the zero special-case and is unlikely to cause visible artifacts (the FIR filter smooths very-low-coverage values before darkening), but it's worth noting for future tuning — a smoothed ramp for very low coverage values (e.g., scaling boost by cov/N for small cov) would produce a gentler transition.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The font pipeline description in .factory/library/architecture.md does not mention stem darkening. The pipeline currently reads 'TrueType parser → glyph outline extraction → scanline rasterizer (2× horizontal + 4× vertical oversampling) → coverage map → gamma-correct alpha blending onto surface' but should include the stem darkening step between coverage map generation and output.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md 'Font Pipeline' section — no mention of stem darkening after FIR filter. Actual pipeline in truetype.rs is: oversample rasterize → downsample → FIR filter → stem darkening LUT → output.

summary: Stem darkening is correctly implemented as specified. The 256-entry compile-time LUT with boost=70 applies equally to all 3 subpixel channels after the FIR filter. Zero stays zero, 255 stays 255, mid-range values are strictly boosted. 7 comprehensive tests pass. Visual verification confirmed heavier, more legible text. Two non-blocking doc comment inaccuracies noted (strict boost range overstated by 3 values, and steep LUT[0]→LUT[1] discontinuity worth noting for future tuning). One shared-state gap: architecture.md font pipeline description should be updated to include the stem darkening step. Overall: pass.
